templates.name: Prometheus remote_write sink
templates.description: Fan-in prometheus.remote_write receiver. Every other metrics pipeline references this one via `prometheus.remote_write.sink.receiver`. Ship this first, everywhere (empty selector).
requires: A Prometheus-compatible `remote_write` endpoint reachable from every collector. Replace the URL below.

templates.name: Alloy self-metrics
templates.description: Scrapes the Alloy process's own /metrics endpoint. Fleet-wide, no selector. Requires the `remote-write-sink` template (or any pipeline that declares `prometheus.remote_write.sink`).
requires: `prometheus.remote_write.sink.receiver` must exist (install the `remote-write-sink` template).

templates.name: Node (Linux host) metrics
templates.description: Embedded node_exporter via `prometheus.exporter.unix`. CPU, memory, disk, network, filesystem, load — the standard host metrics set. Pair with a selector like `collector.os=linux`.
requires: Linux host (won't work on Windows collectors).
`prometheus.remote_write.sink.receiver` must exist (install the `remote-write-sink` template).

templates.name: PostgreSQL metrics
templates.description: Embedded postgres_exporter via `prometheus.exporter.postgres`. Connection string comes from the `POSTGRES_DSN` env var on the collector — set it via Alloy's bootstrap config.
requires: The target collector has the `POSTGRES_DSN` env var set (e.g. `postgresql://user:pass@host:5432/db?sslmode=disable`).
`prometheus.remote_write.sink.receiver` must exist.

templates.name: Elasticsearch metrics
templates.description: Embedded elasticsearch_exporter via `prometheus.exporter.elasticsearch`. Cluster, node, and index-level metrics from an Elasticsearch endpoint.
requires: The target collector has `ELASTICSEARCH_URL` env var set.
`prometheus.remote_write.sink.receiver` must exist.

templates.name: Nginx metrics (via nginx-prometheus-exporter)
templates.description: Scrapes a nginx-prometheus-exporter sidecar. The exporter itself is not bundled in Alloy — deploy it next to nginx (container or systemd) pointing at `stub_status`, and set `NGINX_EXPORTER_ADDR` on the collector.
requires: A running `nginx-prometheus-exporter` reachable from the collector.
Nginx has `stub_status` enabled and the exporter is pointed at it.
The collector has `NGINX_EXPORTER_ADDR` env set (host:port, default `localhost:9113`).
`prometheus.remote_write.sink.receiver` must exist.

templates.name: Kubernetes pod metrics (annotation-driven)
templates.description: Discovers pods via the Kubernetes API and scrapes those annotated with `prometheus.io/scrape=true`. Matches the well-known Prometheus Operator convention.
requires: The Alloy pod runs with a ServiceAccount that has `get/list/watch` on pods (and endpoints/services if you expand the discovery roles).
`prometheus.remote_write.sink.receiver` must exist.

templates.name: systemd journal logs
templates.description: Tails the systemd journal and forwards every entry to Loki. Ideal for catch-all host log collection on Linux systems.
requires: The Alloy process has read access to `/var/log/journal` (typically via the `systemd-journal` group).
A `loki.write` component named `loki.write.sink` (declare one fleet-wide, analogous to `remote-write-sink`).

templates.name: Docker container logs
templates.description: Tails every local Docker container's stdout/stderr via the Docker socket and ships to Loki. Use when collectors run on plain Docker hosts (for Kubernetes, use kubelet / node-level log shipping instead).
requires: `/var/run/docker.sock` mounted into the Alloy container (or Alloy running on the Docker host).
A `loki.write.sink` component must exist.

templates.name: OTLP traces forwarder
templates.description: Accepts OTLP traces (gRPC + HTTP) from local applications and forwards them to a remote OTLP endpoint. Drop this on every app host to get a traces ingress.
requires: The target collector has `OTLP_EXPORT_ENDPOINT` set (e.g. `tempo-gateway:4317`).
Ports 4317 (gRPC) and 4318 (HTTP) available on the host.

templates.name: Loki write sink
templates.description: Fan-in `loki.write` receiver. Every logs pipeline (systemd journal, Docker, k8s pod logs, /var/log files) references this one via `loki.write.sink.receiver`. Install once fleet-wide.
requires: A Loki-compatible push endpoint reachable from every collector. Set `LOKI_WRITE_URL` on the collector or edit the URL inline.

templates.name: Tempo / OTLP traces sink
templates.description: Fan-in `otelcol.exporter.otlp` receiver named `sink`. Trace receivers (OTLP, Zipkin, Jaeger) forward to `otelcol.exporter.otlp.sink.input` so changing the Tempo backend means editing only this pipeline.
requires: A Tempo / OTLP-compatible traces endpoint reachable from every collector. Set `TEMPO_OTLP_ENDPOINT` on the collector (e.g. `tempo:4317`).

templates.name: Mimir / Grafana Cloud remote_write sink
templates.description: Authenticated variant of `remote-write-sink` for Grafana Cloud Metrics, Mimir or Cortex. Has basic_auth + tenant header pre-wired. Install ALONGSIDE `remote-write-sink` (not as a replacement) when you want a managed destination.
requires: `MIMIR_REMOTE_WRITE_URL`, `MIMIR_USER`, `MIMIR_PASS` env vars set on each collector.
If you want to fan-out to BOTH Mimir and your local Prometheus, install this template + the standard `remote-write-sink` and have scrape pipelines `forward_to` both receivers.

templates.name: Redis metrics
templates.description: Embedded redis_exporter via `prometheus.exporter.redis`. Connection info comes from `REDIS_ADDR` (and optionally `REDIS_PASSWORD`) on the collector.
requires: The target collector has `REDIS_ADDR` env set (e.g. `redis://localhost:6379`).
If Redis requires AUTH, also set `REDIS_PASSWORD`.
`prometheus.remote_write.sink.receiver` must exist.

templates.name: MySQL / MariaDB metrics
templates.description: Embedded mysqld_exporter via `prometheus.exporter.mysql`. The DSN is read from the collector's `MYSQL_DSN` env var so secrets never appear in pipeline content.
requires: The target collector has `MYSQL_DSN` env set (e.g. `exporter:pass@tcp(localhost:3306)/`).
The exporter user has the standard `PROCESS, REPLICATION CLIENT, SELECT` grants.
`prometheus.remote_write.sink.receiver` must exist.

templates.name: MongoDB metrics
templates.description: Embedded mongodb_exporter via `prometheus.exporter.mongodb`. The connection URI is read from `MONGODB_URI` on the collector.
requires: The target collector has `MONGODB_URI` env set (e.g. `mongodb://exporter:pass@host:27017/?ssl=false`).
The exporter user has `clusterMonitor` role.
`prometheus.remote_write.sink.receiver` must exist.

templates.name: Windows host metrics
templates.description: Embedded windows_exporter via `prometheus.exporter.windows`. CPU, memory, disk, network, services, IIS — the standard Windows host metrics set. Pair with selector `collector.os=windows`.
requires: Windows host (won't work on Linux collectors).
Alloy is running with sufficient privileges to read perf counters (typically LocalSystem or admin).
`prometheus.remote_write.sink.receiver` must exist.

templates.name: Container metrics (cAdvisor)
templates.description: Embedded cAdvisor via `prometheus.exporter.cadvisor`. Per-container CPU, memory, network, and FS metrics from the local container runtime. Ideal for Docker hosts; on Kubernetes prefer the kubelet's built-in cAdvisor (scrape via service discovery).
requires: Alloy can read `/var/lib/docker` and `/sys/fs/cgroup` (mount them into the container if Alloy itself runs in Docker).
`prometheus.remote_write.sink.receiver` must exist.

templates.name: Blackbox synthetic probes
templates.description: Embedded blackbox_exporter via `prometheus.exporter.blackbox`. Defines HTTP/TCP probe modules and a starter target list — extend the targets array to monitor your own URLs/services.
requires: `prometheus.remote_write.sink.receiver` must exist.

templates.name: Kafka metrics
templates.description: Embedded kafka_exporter via `prometheus.exporter.kafka`. Reports broker, topic, partition, and consumer-group metrics. Configure brokers via `KAFKA_BROKER` (single) or edit the array inline (multi-broker).
requires: The target collector has `KAFKA_BROKER` env set (e.g. `localhost:9092`), or edit the inline `kafka_uris` array.
`prometheus.remote_write.sink.receiver` must exist.

templates.name: RabbitMQ metrics (native plugin / sidecar)
templates.description: Scrapes RabbitMQ metrics. Two valid sources: (1) the native `rabbitmq_prometheus` plugin on port 15692 (RabbitMQ 3.8+, recommended), or (2) the standalone rabbitmq_exporter sidecar. Set `RABBITMQ_METRICS_ADDR` to the chosen endpoint.
requires: Either RabbitMQ's `rabbitmq_prometheus` plugin enabled (default port 15692), or a `rabbitmq_exporter` reachable from the collector.
The collector has `RABBITMQ_METRICS_ADDR` env set (host:port).
`prometheus.remote_write.sink.receiver` must exist.

templates.name: Per-process metrics
templates.description: Embedded process_exporter via `prometheus.exporter.process`. Per-process CPU, memory, and IO metrics. The matcher block scopes which processes are reported — keep it tight to avoid metric explosion.
requires: Linux host with /proc readable by Alloy (the default).
`prometheus.remote_write.sink.receiver` must exist.

templates.name: Kubernetes object metrics (kube-state-metrics)
templates.description: Discovers the kube-state-metrics service in `kube-system` and scrapes its `http-metrics` port. Reports state for Deployments, Pods, Nodes, etc. — the metrics that complement raw kubelet/cAdvisor stats.
requires: kube-state-metrics deployed in `kube-system` exposing the `http-metrics` port (default upstream chart).
Alloy's ServiceAccount has `get/list/watch` on `endpoints` in `kube-system`.
`prometheus.remote_write.sink.receiver` must exist.

templates.name: Generic /var/log file tail
templates.description: Tails arbitrary log files under `/var/log` and ships them to Loki. Adjust the include glob to scope down. Useful for catch-all log collection on Linux hosts that don't speak journald.
requires: Alloy has read access to the configured paths (typically root or the `adm` group on Debian/Ubuntu).
A `loki.write.sink` component must exist (install the `loki-write-sink` template).

templates.name: Kubernetes pod logs
templates.description: Discovers every pod via the Kubernetes API and tails each container's logs from the node's `/var/log/pods` tree. Surface namespace, pod, and container as Loki labels. Run as a DaemonSet.
requires: Alloy runs as a DaemonSet with `/var/log/pods` mounted into the container.
Alloy's ServiceAccount has `get/list/watch` on `pods`.
A `loki.write.sink` component must exist (install the `loki-write-sink` template).

templates.name: Windows Event Log
templates.description: Tails a Windows Event Log channel (`Application` by default) and ships entries to Loki. Pair with selector `collector.os=windows`. Add additional `loki.source.windowsevent` blocks to capture more channels (System, Security, etc.).
requires: Windows host (won't work on Linux collectors).
Alloy has permission to read the channel — typically requires LocalSystem or membership in `Event Log Readers`.
A `loki.write.sink` component must exist (install the `loki-write-sink` template).

templates.name: Zipkin traces receiver
templates.description: Accepts Zipkin v2 traces on `:9411` and forwards them to the OTLP traces sink. Pairs with the `tempo-otlp-sink` template (or any other `otelcol.exporter.otlp` named `sink`) to give you a Zipkin ingress without a separate gateway.
requires: Port 9411 available on the host.
An `otelcol.exporter.otlp` named `sink` must exist (install the `tempo-otlp-sink` template).
